texto: I can go years thinking that it seems impossible that I will ever satisfy that appetite again and then it is easy to satisfy and no one notices or cares, nor does it make me happy, when loneliness surrounds me like water I've already drowned in without dying.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Francisco Goldman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I can go years thinking that it seems impossible that I will ever satisfy that appetite again and…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, loneliness, satisfy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
